nodes.description: Biallelic PEX7 variants impair peroxisomal import of PTS2-containing proteins, causing a Refsum phenotype in a subset of patients by disrupting peroxisomal alpha-oxidation machinery.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular basis of Refsum disease: sequence variations in phytanoyl-CoA hydroxylase (PHYH) and the PTS2 receptor (PEX7).
evidence.snippet: two genes, PHYH (also named PAHX) and PEX7, have been identified to cause Refsum disease

nodes.description: Biallelic PHYH variants impair phytanoyl-CoA 2-hydroxylase, the peroxisomal enzyme catalyzing the first alpha-oxidation step in phytanic acid degradation.

evidence.reference_title: Structure of human phytanoyl-CoA 2-hydroxylase identifies molecular mechanisms of Refsum disease.
evidence.snippet: mutations in phytanoyl-CoA 2-hydroxylase (PAHX), an Fe(II) and 2-oxoglutarate (2OG)-dependent oxygenase that catalyzes the initial alpha-oxidation step in the degradation of phytenic acid in peroxisomes.

nodes.description: Long-term management restricts phytanic-acid-rich foods, avoids fasting and sudden weight loss, and maintains adequate calories to reduce mobilization of stored phytanic acid.
